tl_items.title: As AI Investments Surge, CEOs Take the Lead
tl_items.snippet: Nearly three-quarters of CEOs are their company’s chief decision maker on AI. Most of them remain bullish on the technology.

tl_items.title: 10 Wealth Management Trends For 2026
tl_items.snippet: A forward look at key 2026 wealth management trends, including AI-driven advice, tokenized cash, private markets, embedded wealth, and downturn readiness.

tl_items.title: How Boards Can Lead in a World Remade by AI
tl_items.snippet: In brief AI’s impacts on strategy, talent, and risk make it essential for boards to adapt their oversight approaches. tl_items.snippet: Wharton's Ethan Mollick discusses the current state of AI and the key implications for business, labor, and society in the year ahead.

tl_items.title: AI Rewires Brains: Tom Slater's Insightful Essay | Pete Matthew posted on the topic | LinkedIn
tl_items.snippet: This is the best thing I’ve read on AI in ages - a brilliant essay by Tom Slater of Scottish Mortgage Investment Trust (whom I’m unable to tag) about how AI is physically rewiring our brains. It is immaculately written, very easy to digest and might be the most important thing you read this year.

tl_items.title: Algorithmic Revolution: AI Leads Investment Strategies in 2026
tl_items.snippet: Industry leaders predict AI will revolutionize business by 2026. IBM believes quantum computers will surpass classical computers that year, which could lead to breakthroughs in many industries.

tl_items.title: Q4 2025 Investment AI Trends: Traditional AI Leads Adoption, But GenAI to Reshape the Status Quo in 2026
tl_items.snippet: Investment firms moved past pilots in generative AI, but trust and privacy concerns restrain live rollouts. Agentic AI remains in exploration mode.

tl_items.title: GenAI Strategy & Investment Management | Portal26 Platform Feature
tl_items.snippet: Balance GenAI investments across private, public, and licensed AI with Portal26's strategy management. Optimize ROI, manage risk, and align with enterprise objectives and policies.

blocked_items.title: Citi Deploys 4 AI Tools Across Wealth Division, Automating Notes, Research Queries and Client 360 Views
blocked_items.reason: Fabrication detected: Client 360 rollout timeline contradiction: Entry states 'full rollout planned for April 2026', but source states full rollout to Citi Private Bank and Wealth at Work is planned for 'this month' (April 2026) with ALL Wealth colleagues gaining access in Q2 2026 — the entry omits the Q2 2026 phase and mischaracterizes the rollout scope.; Portfolio Intelligence global rollout: Entry states 'global rollout through year-end', but source specifies expanding to Private Bank clients globally in Q2 first, then all Wealth clients by year-end — the entry collapses these two phases into one.; CitiScribe description: Entry states it 'automates meeting note-taking', but source describes it as 'automates note-taking, summarizing client discussions' — minor but the entry's characterization is consistent enough to not be a hard fail.; Citi Stylus Workspaces launch: Entry states 'launched in September 2025' which is consistent with source reference to 'Last September' (published April 2026).
